request.system: You are a regulatory analyst. Given a prediction-market contract and a single regulatory news record, decide if this record is relevant to whether the contract resolves YES or NO. Score relevance 0-10 (0 = off-topic, 10 = directly determinative). Return JSON matching the schema. one_line_why should be ≤160 chars and explain how this record moves resolution probability. Also judge directionality: does this event make YES resolution more likely (bullish), less likely (bearish), or neither (neutral)? Judge magnitude: high = materially changes probability, medium = notable but not decisive, low = incremental signal. Judge timeline shift: does this event suggest resolution will come sooner or later than expected, or no change (none)?
request.user:
Contract title: Will the US enter a recession in 2026?
Resolution criteria: Resolves YES if the National Bureau of Economic Research (NBER) Business Cycle Dating Committee announces that the United States entered an economic recession with a peak month in 2026, on or before 2026-12-31. Resolves NO otherwise.
Conditions:
  A: NBER 2026-peak recession by 2026-12-31 — The NBER Business Cycle Dating Committee publicly announces that the U.S. entered a recession with the peak month occurring in 2026, and this announcement is made on or before 2026-12-31.
Settlement entities: ['National Bureau of Economic Research', 'Federal Reserve System', 'Federal Open Market Committee', 'Department of the Treasury', 'Bureau of Labor Statistics', 'Bureau of Economic Analysis', 'Council of Economic Advisers']

Record title: Basel III monitoring report
Record date: 2026-03-24
Record regulator: Basel Committee on Banking Supervision
Record topic: Basel Committee on Banking Supervision
Record entities: ['Australian Prudential Regulation Authority', 'Bank for International Settlements', 'Bank of England', 'Bank of Italy', 'Bank of Japan', 'Bank of Mexico', 'Bank of Spain', 'Basel Committee on Banking Supervision', 'Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System', 'Central Bank of Argentina', 'Central Bank of Brazil', 'China Banking and Insurance Regulatory Commission', 'Commission de Surveillance du Secteur Financier', 'De Nederlandsche Bank', 'Deutsche Bundesbank', 'European Banking Authority', 'European Central Bank', 'European Central Bank Single Supervisory Mechanism', 'European Commission', 'Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation', 'Federal Financial Supervisory Authority', 'Federal Reserve Bank of New York', 'Financial Services Agency', 'Financial Supervisory Service', 'Finnish Financial Supervisory Authority', 'Finansinspektionen', 'Hong Kong Monetary Authority', 'Indonesia Financial Services Authority', 'Martin Birn', 'Monetary Authority of Singapore', 'National Bank of Belgium', 'National Banking and Securities Commission', 'Office of the Comptroller of the Currency', 'Office of the Superintendent of Financial Institutions', 'Prudential Regulation Authority', 'Reserve Bank of India', 'Saudi Central Bank', 'South African Reserve Bank', 'Swiss Financial Market Supervisory Authority']

response.one_line_why: Basel III monitoring report covers bank regulatory metrics, not NBER dating; it does not directly affect whether NBER will date a 2026 recession.
